verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 gives the immediate next action, “tell me in your own words,” then names “Where the app lives” and “What it's built with”; B opens with meta-commentary, “question prompt got dismissed,” before the useful ask.
verdict.decisiveness: A is stronger but still not fully decisive: it promises “a recommendation for your stack”; B mainly defers with “Once I can see the code and know those.”
verdict.fabrication: B invents context with “since it's not under `/private/tmp` or `/tmp`”; A has no material invented fact beyond conversational framing.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it asks for only two inputs and gives a concrete plan shape: “Approach,” “Data model,” “Flows,” “Sessions,” “Routes/UI,” “Security baseline,” “Rollout.”
